pages.raw_text:
Edward ]. Cannon, division coun-
sci of the Northern Pacific, will
next Monday formally submit to
Mayor Moore the plans for pro-
posed grade separation. These
plans have been back and forth be-
tween Spokane and st. Paul for
several months and are now in
shape the company seems satisfied
with. Until submitted to the may
or, city officials will not attempt
to pass judgment upon them. May
or Moore will submit them to the
council, which will in turn refer
them back to the city engineer
and board of works. The plans as
finally designed are practically as
previously published in The Press.
The grade will be elevated upon
concrete fill with steel viaducts
over the streets. Considerable dis
cusston among councilmen is ex-
pected over the lowering of the
street grades in various places.
